headline: TSX gains as investors weigh positive U.S. jobs data against Middle East fears
summary: Investing.com -- Canada’s primary stock index has risen on Friday as market participants balanced a robust U.S. labor report against renewed military activity in the Strait of Hormuz. By 12:10 pm ET, the S&P/TSX Composite index has climbed 166.24 points, or 0.5%, to 34,021.87 by mid-morning. The blue-chip S&P/TSX 60 index also saw gains, increasing 9.15 points or 0.5% to 1,974.22. Investors appeared heartened by economic resilience south of the border, even as geopolitical risks resurfaced in en

headline: What Might The Fed Do After April's Red Hot Inflation Report
summary: Nasdaq QQQs reached an all-time high as April's job growth and steady unemployment validate the Fed's decision to hold rates. Read full analysis here.

headline: Market concentration at records: Will easing Iran tensions spark rotation?
summary: Investing.com -- U.S. equity market concentration has surpassed previous historical peaks, with the top five and top 25 companies now accounting for approximately 30% and 53% of the overall market, respectively, according to Wolfe Research.

raw.headline: Market concentration at records: Will easing Iran tensions spark rotation?

headline: S&P 500 rally depends on participation beyond semis and easing Hormuz risks
summary: Investing.com -- Global equity markets hit fresh highs this week on hopes of a U.S.-Iran peace deal, but Barclays strategist Emmanuel Cau warned that a broader and sustained rally remains contingent on the Strait of Hormuz reopening and participation extending beyond an increasingly stretched semiconductors trade.
